subject: Economics
chapter: Forms of Market and Price Determination

formulas.name: Law of Demand
formulas.formula: Demand curve slopes downward to the right
formulas.notes: Represents the inverse relationship between price and quantity demanded

formulas.name: Law of Supply
formulas.formula: Supply curve slopes upward to the right
formulas.notes: Represents the direct relationship between price and quantity supplied

formulas.name: Equilibrium Price (P*)
formulas.formula: P* = (Price Elasticity of Demand + 1) / (Price Elasticity of Supply + 1)
formulas.variables: Price Elasticity of Demand (PED), Price Elasticity of Supply (PES)
formulas.notes: Used to calculate the equilibrium price in a market

formulas.name: Equilibrium Quantity (Q*)
formulas.notes: Used to calculate the equilibrium quantity in a market

formulas.name: Price Elasticity of Demand (PED)
formulas.formula: PED = % change in QD / % change in P
formulas.variables: Percentage change in Quantity Demanded (%ΔQD), Percentage change in Price (%ΔP)
formulas.notes: Measures the responsiveness of quantity demanded to changes in price

formulas.name: Price Elasticity of Supply (PES)
formulas.formula: PES = % change in QS / % change in P
formulas.variables: Percentage change in Quantity Supplied (%ΔQS), Percentage change in Price (%ΔP)
formulas.notes: Measures the responsiveness of quantity supplied to changes in price

formulas.name: Monopoly Price
formulas.notes: Used to calculate the price of a monopolist
